name: Juniper Mist AI-Driven Network API
what_it_does: Juniper Mist AI-driven networking REST API for cloud-native wireless, wired, and WAN management platform. Enables AI agents to manage site and device configuration and deployment automation, handle AI-powered network anomaly detection and event processing, access wireless client experience and SLE (Service Level Expectation) analytics, retrieve Marvis AI virtual network assistant query results, manage WLAN, switch, and gateway policy configuration, handle network topology and inventory management, access real-time and historical performance analytics, retrieve user and client troubleshooting data, manage SD-WAN and WAN Assurance configuration, and integrate Mist AI network events with ITSM, SIEM, and network operations platforms.
use_cases: Querying Marvis AI for network troubleshooting from IT helpdesk agents
Managing campus network configuration from network operations agents
Retrieving SLE metrics and anomaly events from NOC monitoring agents
Automating network onboarding from IT provisioning agents
not_for: Data center spine-leaf networking without campus WiFi focus
Consumer home networking without enterprise multi-site management
Perimeter firewall without wireless and campus network context
best_when: An enterprise using Juniper Mist wants AI agents to automate network operations, leverage Marvis AI for troubleshooting, monitor SLE metrics, manage device configuration, and integrate with ITSM and NOC platforms.
avoid_when: OPERATIONAL RISK: Automated AP, switch, or gateway configuration changes deploy immediately to production devices — test in lab sites first; bulk site configuration pushes require change management. Automated WLAN policy changes can impact connected users and IoT devices.

auth.notes: Mist uses API token authentication (user-level and org-level tokens). OAuth 2.0 available for partner integrations. Mist Automation Programmability GitHub for SDK and examples. Python mistapi SDK for automation. Webhooks for real-time event streaming. Well-documented REST API with interactive docs at api.mist.com. Org vs site token scoping for access control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-AP annual subscription with AI features
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual per-device subscription; Mist AI included; WAN Assurance and Premium Analytics add-ons
pricing.notes: Sunnyvale, California. Mist Systems acquired by Juniper Networks (2019, $405M). Juniper acquired by HP/HPE (2024, $14B). AI-native networking pioneer. Marvis virtual network assistant. WiFi 6/6E and Bluetooth/IoT sensor integration. Strong higher education and healthcare verticals. Competes with Cisco Meraki and Aruba for AI-driven campus networking.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per org token; well-documented in API docs; webhook preferred for real-time data

known_agent_gotchas: OPERATIONAL RISK: Configuration changes deploy immediately to production sites — use test sites or staging orgs; Mist supports multiple org contexts for isolation
Org vs site token scoping — org-level tokens have broad access; prefer site-scoped tokens for least-privilege automation
Marvis API — Marvis AI query API allows natural-language network troubleshooting; powerful but requires understanding of Mist data model
Webhook event volumes — high-traffic environments generate high webhook volumes; implement appropriate filtering and buffering
HPE acquisition context — Juniper acquired by HPE (2024); product roadmap and API may evolve; verify current API surface
mistapi Python SDK — Juniper maintains well-documented Python SDK with examples on GitHub Mist-Automation-Programmability org
agent_readiness.security_notes: AI-driven campus networking. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, FedRAMP. API token/OAuth2. US/EU/APAC. Network topology, device config, and client analytics.

security_score_components.security_notes: AI campus networking. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. API token/OAuth2. Multi-region. Network config and analytics data.
